summary: Wired Lane 2 of the UI Regression Plan. Added a new XCUITest class TodayContinueRegression alongside the existing OwloryUITests smoke class in owlory_xcode/OwloryUITests/OwloryUITests.swift, plus a new Makefile target make ui-regression that runs only that class against /tmp/owlory-ui-regression-derived-data. The smoke target is unchanged and still scopes to -only-testing:OwloryUITests/OwloryUITests, so the regression batch does not run on every `make ui-smoke`. First batch covers all six Continue source visibility paths, four source-derived routing paths (Home task, active Home protocol run, due-today Training, in-progress Writing), and three Focus row actions (Done, Defer, Drop). Initial coverage overlaps with the smoke set by design; the lane is the deliverable and future slices grow the regression class with edge cases.

contract_status_changes.before: Lane 2 (regression suite) was defined in docs/workflows/ui-regression-plan.md but `make ui-regression` was documented intent only; no XCUITest class existed for the regression batch.
contract_status_changes.after: `make ui-regression` is wired and runs OwloryUITests/TodayContinueRegression against /tmp/owlory-ui-regression-derived-data. The regression class lives in the same file as the smoke class (because the Xcode project's pbxproj is outside this slice's allowed_paths and uses traditional PBXGroup file references rather than synchronized folders); the `-only-testing` filter keeps the smoke and regression invocations independent. Smoke command unchanged.

contract_status_changes.before: docs/product/domains/today.md and docs/workflows/roadmap-status.md described Continue smoke coverage and the regression lane as queued behind owlory-ui-regression-batch-1-today-continue.
contract_status_changes.after: today.md records that the regression batch exists in parallel to the smoke, with the same matrix coverage. roadmap-status.md's UI regression / snapshot row now notes the wired make ui-regression target and the first batch's scope. ui-testing-hygiene.md gains a Regression Batch section pointing at the new command. The five-lane plan is amended so Lane 2's gating-commands paragraph names `make ui-regression` as wired (not documented intent).

residual_risks: The regression class lives in the same .swift file as the smoke class because Xcode's project.pbxproj is outside this slice's allowed_paths and the project uses traditional PBXGroup file references (not synchronized folders). A future slice that owns the pbxproj can split the regression class into its own file or its own test target without changing the make target or the -only-testing filter, since both match by class name.

Initial regression coverage overlaps with the smoke set by design (six visibility tests, four routing tests, three Focus row actions). The lane is the deliverable; future slices grow the regression class with edge cases (mixed Continue lists with multiple seeded sources, Continue cap behavior, error paths). Until that growth happens, `make ui-regression` and `make ui-smoke` provide near-identical assertions and the regression batch's distinct value comes only from running on a different cadence.

Routing and visibility regression for `focusItem` and `carriedFocusItem` remain N/A by contract (covered transitively through the source-derived routing and action tests per the deferred-coverage slice classification). If a future surface decouples those code paths, this regression class must be extended explicitly rather than relying on the transitive coverage claim.
No screenshot/device/TestFlight proof is claimed by this slice. Those lanes are owned by future slices per the UI Regression Plan.
